title: Documentation Claims
description: Claims extracted from documentation files inside the repository, each resolved against the discovered graphs. Docs propose, the repository proves: a claim never instantiates an accepted semantic node by itself. Stored at .work/semantic-loop/doc-claims.json. Optional artefact — absent when the doc-alignment phase found no documentation (recorded as an explicit unknown, never a gate failure).

extracted_by.description: parser_id of the registered claim extractor (see parser-registry.json). Extraction is model-driven interpretation; registering it makes that fallibility visible.

documents.description: Documentation files that were read for claims, whether or not any claim was extracted from them.

claim_type.description: terminology = business term for a construct; feature = user-visible capability; rule = business/validation/authorization rule; boundary = module/integration partitioning; intent = the why behind a construct; data = meaning of stored data.

text.description: The claim as normalized by the extractor, e.g. 'users can export the activity report as CSV'.

excerpt.description: Verbatim quote from the document that the claim was read from. Must appear within the cited span — the verifier re-resolves it, so a human can always check the reading.

file.description: Repository-relative path of the documentation file. Must be inside the repository; external sources must be snapshotted in first.

status.description: confirmed = parsed evidence supports it (mapped_nodes required); contradicted = parsed evidence refutes it (a conflict must be recorded on the affected node, or the claim stands alone as drift); unverifiable = nothing in the repository can settle it.

mapped_nodes.description: Ids of semantic-graph nodes this claim maps onto. Required non-empty for confirmed claims.

status_evidence.description: How the status was established: which nodes/files confirm it, what contradicts it, or what was searched without result.
